name: Changelog Generator
description: Generates a Keep a Changelog style entry from git history between two refs, grouping commits into Added / Changed / Fixed / Removed / Security and flagging breaking changes.
author: ClaudeStudio Docs Pack

inputs.label: From ref / tag
inputs.help: Starting point, e.g. the previous release tag. Leave empty to use the latest tag.

inputs.label: Version to label this entry
inputs.help: e.g. 1.4.0. Used as the section heading.

steps.title: Collect commits
steps.prompt: Run git to list commits between {{from_ref}} (default: the most recent tag if empty) and {{to_ref}}, including subjects, bodies, and merged PR titles where available. Use a format that exposes each commit message in full.

steps.title: Classify changes
steps.prompt: Classify each commit into Keep a Changelog categories: Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, Security. If style = {{style}} is 'conventional', map types (feat -> Added, fix -> Fixed, perf/refactor -> Changed, etc.) and detect breaking changes via '!' or 'BREAKING CHANGE'. Otherwise infer from wording. Drop noise (merge commits, formatting-only, dependency bumps unless security-relevant). Rewrite each entry as a clear, user-facing line.

steps.title: Write changelog entry
steps.prompt: Produce a Markdown changelog entry headed '## [{{version}}] - {{date}}', grouped by category in Keep a Changelog order, listing only non-empty categories. Put a clearly marked 'Breaking Changes' callout at the top if any exist. Output only the new entry so it can be prepended to CHANGELOG.md.
